title: Cleanspark Inc (CLSK) Q2 2026 Earnings Call Highlights: Navigating Challenges with Strategic Growth
text: Cleanspark reported mixed results for Q2 2026. The company generated $136 million in revenue, representing a 25% decline from the previous quarter. Gross margins contracted to over 40% from 47% sequentially. The firm posted a net loss of approximately $378 million, though this figure included $263 million in noncash charges related to Bitcoin mark-to-market adjustments. Adjusted EBITDA improved to negative $241 million compared to negative $295 million in the prior quarter. Bitcoin mining output totaled 1,799 coins, down 22 units from the previous quarter. Power costs decreased to $0.052 per kilowatt hour from $0.056. The company maintains almost $1.2 billion in liquidity, with $260 million in cash and 13,561 Bitcoin valued at $925 million. Digital asset management generated approximately $4 million in cash returns for the quarter. Management highlighted infrastructure expansion, noting the company has expanded its contracted capacity to 1.8 gigawatts, with plans for further growth. An additional 5+ gigawatts of potential capacity remains in discussions. The organization is pivoting toward AI and HPC infrastructure, with plans to convert existing Bitcoin mining sites like Sandersville, Brazoria, and Sealy. Leadership identified labor bottlenecks, especially in Texas, but proposed mitigating this through factory-based data center construction that reduces on-site labor requirements by approximately 70%.
